mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and drive 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants price in prolonged rate persistence.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=4.33, CPIAUCSL=321.5 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 55% of instances when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending capacity. This demand destruction flows through to lower inflation expectations and equity earnings, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth concerns dominate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE=4.1%, baseline for detecting deterioration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of instances when unemployment increases by > 0.4pp, or forward equity returns are positive in > 60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates demand contraction. This signals recession risk, prompting flight-to-safety flows into Treasuries and downward pressure on 10-year yields.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 1200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=715417, HOUST=1256 near trigger zone
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of instances when both demand indicators weaken simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 104.0, signaling robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This reduces inflation pressures and supports real yields, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as growth expectations strengthen without inflation concerns.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in > 50% of periods when INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand net new jobs per month while UNRATE remains stable. This signals labor market softening without immediate unemployment spike, creating uncertainty that depresses equity valuations and drives 10-year Treasury yields lower.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100 thousand AND UNRATE stable within ±0.1pp
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of instances when payroll growth slows with stable unemployment, or forward equity returns exceed baseline by > 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 320. This stagflationary signal creates policy uncertainty and reduces real asset returns, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth concerns override inflation expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of stagflationary instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window in low-growth, high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines below 4.0% while CPIAUCSL inflation falls below 315, signaling monetary easing cycle. Lower real rates compress equity discount rates and reduce Treasury yields as market prices in sustained accommodative policy.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=4.33, CPIAUCSL=321.5 above easing threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 55% of easing periods, or forward equity returns are negative in > 50% of instances when both easing conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth slows below 50 thousand. This dual supply-labor weakness signals recession risk, triggering flight-to-quality flows that compress 10-year Treasury yields downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly decline > 0.5% AND PAYEMS monthly change < 50 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in > 50% of instances when both supply and labor indicators weaken simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.
